funfacts.sentence: Nacho Ferri is Westerlo's top scorer with 11 goals. Rafiki Said has scored 7 times for Standard.
Bryan Reynolds has more yellow cards (8) than any other player at Westerlo. Marco Ilaimaharitra has collected 9 for Standard.
Standard have lost none of their last 7 away matches.

ai_preview.text:
**Standard Liège rolls into Westerlo looking to extend their strong recent form**, and the visitors have serious injury depth issues at home to worry about. 🔴

Westerlo are hovering around .500 lately—two wins but also two losses in their last five—while **Standard have found their groove with two wins and two draws**, including a ruthless 5-0 demolition of Royal Antwerp. The head-to-head is split, so no edge there, but Standard's attacking teeth look sharper right now.

Key storylines:

- **Standard's attack vs. Westerlo's thin backline**: Missing E. Destan and I. Sakamoto forces the hosts to get creative, and Standard's T. Nkada and B. Nguene will fancy the space.
- **Standard's missing four**: D. Eckert Ayensa, J. Homawoo, M. Ilaimaharitra, and M. E. Hankouri out is brutal—bench depth will be tested if this gets tight.
- **N. Bohamdi-Kamoni's form**: Westerlo need their forward to turn up in big moments here.

Expected lineups:

- **KVC Westerlo**: A. Jungdal, B. Reynolds, E. Bayram, S. Kimura, R. Neustädter, D. Haspolat, A. Piedfort, N. Ferri, J. Alcócer, A. Patrão, N. Bohamdi-Kamoni
- **Standard Liège**: M. Epolo, I. Hautekiet, H. Lawrence, G. Mortensen, D. Bates, R. Saïd, A. Abid, I. Karamoko, T. Nkada, B. Nguene, T. Henry

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 1.5**: Over 1.5 goals

Prediction:

**Standard Liège 2–1 KVC Westerlo**. Standard edge this, but Westerlo will scrap.

funfacts.sentence: Barracas haven't won in their last 6 games.
Marco Collao has more yellow cards (2) than any other player at Audax. Damian Martinez has collected 3 for Barracas.
Audax have played 6 home matches in a row without winning.

ai_preview.text:
**A rematch nobody really wanted but the Copa Sudamericana demands.** Audax Italiano and Barracas Central square off again just weeks after they drew 1-1, and honestly? Both teams are struggling to find rhythm 🎯

Audax have won once in five, while Barracas are winless in their last five games — this has "neither team deserves to advance" energy. The H2H is locked at a draw, and we're getting the same defensive setup vibes as last time.

Key storylines:

- **Audax's attacking trio** of **M. Vadulli**, **G. Chiaverano**, and **P. Guajardo** need to break through a Barracas backline that's been stingy but not clinical
- **Barracas' 5-3-2 shape** is built to absorb pressure; **J. Espínola** will get work if Audax commits numbers forward
- Coin flip energy — neither side has the form to impose themselves, so expect caution and set pieces

Expected lineups:

- **Audax Italiano**: T. Ahumada, E. Matus, D. Piña, M. Ortíz, R. Rebolledo, E. Ferrario, M. Collao, F. Mateos, M. Vadulli, G. Chiaverano, P. Guajardo
- **Barracas Central**: J. Espínola, F. Tobio, N. Demartini, N. Capraro, R. Insúa, D. Martínez, I. Tapia, T. Porra, D. Miloc, J. Candia, N. Briasco

Best picks:

- **Total Goals 3.5**: Under 3.5 goals

Prediction:

**1-1 draw** — both teams park the bus and call it a day.
